channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: Sullivan steps down at West Ham in wake of historical allegations
item.description: West Ham United co-owner David Sullivan steps down as joint chairman of the club with immediate effect following a joint investigation by BBC Panorama and the Times newspaper into his behaviour.

item.title: Mr Irreplaceable and Ballon d'Or contender - is this Kane's time?
item.description: Harry Kane has been described as irreplaceable for England, but will the World Cup provide the stage for him to prove he is now the best?

item.title: Lightning halts pre-World Cup friendly in Texas for two hours
item.description: Saudi Arabia's World Cup warm-up game against Puerto Rico in Texas is halted for nearly two hours because of extreme weather.

item.title: The pioneer in an unlikely World Cup team
item.description: When Desmond Armstrong faced the media at the World Cup in 1990, the first question he got was one that would stick in his mind for the wrong reasons.

item.title: Arsenal approach PSG defender Mbemba
item.description: Arsenal make an approach to sign teenage Paris St-Germain defender Emmanuel Mbemba on a free transfer this summer.

item.title: Spain expose England's flaws in painful defeat
item.description: England just needed to avoid defeat against Spain - instead they suffered their heaviest loss in 17 years, but it is not time to panic yet.

item.title: Republic of Ireland rescue draw against Canada
item.description: Chiedozie Ogbene scores a second half equaliser for the Republic of Ireland in a 1-1 draw against Canada in Montreal.

item.title: The underrated impact of Scotland's back-shaving hype man
item.description: Liam Kelly looks perplexed after being told he has been singled out by some team-mates as Scotland's "hype man".

item.title: Tuchel will not let Tampa pitch affect selection
item.description: England head coach Thomas Tuchel says reports about the state of the pitch at the venue for his side's friendly against New Zealand will not influence team selection.

item.title: O'Neill set to stay on as Celtic manager
item.description: Martin O'Neill will continue as Celtic manager after leading the side to a Premiership and Scottish Cup double.

item.title: How to follow the World Cup on the BBC
item.description: The biggest World Cup in history starts on 11 June and there are more ways to follow it on the BBC than ever before.

item.title: How to watch the World Cup on the BBC and ITV
item.description: The BBC will broadcast Scotland's first World Cup match since 1998, when they take on Haiti on 14 June, plus their final group match against Brazil and England's group match against Ghana.

item.title: World Cup 2026: All 48 squads confirmed
item.description: All 48 confirmed squads for the 2026 World Cup in the US, Canada and Mexico.

item.title: Download your World Cup 2026 wallchart
item.description: Get your essential piece of kit now by downloading the Fifa World Cup 2026 wallchart.

item.title: Which World Cup team are you? Take our quiz
item.description: Whether you're a neutral in need of a team to support or just fancy following a second side, BBC Sport has got you covered.

item.title: Iran says staff blocked from entering US after players given World Cup visas
item.description: Iranian officials say technical staff have been denied visas, hours after the US said players could enter the country.

item.title: Will transfers be a distraction for England at World Cup?
item.description: England's World Cup campaign is also in the middle of a busy summer transfer window, but how much of a distraction is it?

item.title: Paraguay star Enciso injured in World Cup warm-up
item.description: Paraguay forward Julio Enciso's World Cup is in doubt after he was injured during a 4-0 friendly win over Nicaragua.

item.title: Fifa clarifies water bottle stadium rule
item.description: Fifa says fans will be able to bring sealed disposable water bottles into World Cup stadiums following criticism over a decision to ban reusable bottles.

item.title: The story of Norway's Viking World Cup photoshoot
item.description: Renowned British photographer David Yarrow tells BBC Sport what inspired his viral Norway World Cup send-off photo.

item.title: How thunderstorms and lightning could impact games at World Cup
item.description: With several host cities in peak thunderstorm season there is a high chance that some World Cup games could be disrupted. Here is how and why.

item.title: 'The things that forge them are similar' - the origins of Messi & Ronaldo
item.description: Both from humble beginnings, BBC Sport looks at how Lionel Messi and Cristiano Ronaldo became two of the greatest footballers of all time.

item.title: Watch: Weir hat-trick as Scotland thump Israel
item.description: Watch as Scotland thump Israel 6-0 in World Cup qualifying Group B4, with captain Caroline Weir scoring a hat-trick.

item.title: Highlights: Wales held to draw in Montenegro
item.description: Watch highlights as Montenegro and Wales draw in Podgorica in Women's World Cup qualifying.

item.title: Watch: When Hagi's Romania thrashed Wales
item.description: Wales travel to Bucharest to face Romania on Saturday for the first time since their World Cup qualifying defeat in 1992.

item.title: Messi or Ronaldo - who is the greatest of all time?
item.description: Some of the greats of the game debate the long-asked question: Lionel Messi or Cristiano Ronaldo?

item.title: Germany forward Karl ruled out of World Cup
item.description: Germany forward Lennart Karl is ruled out of the 2026 World Cup with a thigh injury.

item.title: Why is Fifa giving clubs $355m during the World Cup?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains why clubs are being given a share of $355m by Fifa for having players involved in the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: Advantage Scotland as they await Cuthbert injury news
item.description: Scotland stay top of their World Cup qualifying group on goal difference from Belgium amid fears of a serious injury to key midfielder Erin Cuthbert.

item.title: McLeish given St Mirren chance - how have young Premiership coaches fared?
item.description: St Mirren have chosen youth over experience in sticking with interim manager Craig McLeish in the long term, but how have other young managers fared in the Scottish Premiership?

item.title: Mowbray returns as Blackburn head coach
item.description: Blackburn Rovers appoint Tony Mowbray as their new head coach for a second spell in charge.

item.title: Macclesfield FC co-owner suspended following arrest
item.description: Robert Smethurst, 48, has been "temporarily relieved of his duties", the National League North club said.

item.title: Bellamy urges Wales to dominate Romania
item.description: Head coach Craig Bellamy wants Wales team to try to dominate in Saturday's friendly against Romania in Bucharest.

item.title: Watch 24 Hours with Ella Toone on BBC iPlayer
item.description: Watch the trailer for a new BBC Sport documentary offering unprecedented behind-the-scenes access to Manchester United and England star Ella Toone as she prepares for her wedding, while still coming to terms with the loss of her father.

item.title: Shearer on Beckham, pressure and Argentina heartbreak
item.description: Alan Shearer reflects on his and England's performances at the 1998 World Cup.

item.title: Robodogs and helicopters: One Mexican city's approach to World Cup security
item.description: In the shadow of cartel violence, BBC’s Will Grant got a first-hand look at security plans in Monterrey.

item.title: Diana Ross & 'GOAT' Ronaldo - Richards watches World Cup moments
item.description: Former England defender and current pundit Micah Richards looks at some of the most memorable World Cup moments.

item.title: Rivals: Messi v Ronaldo - watch the trailer
item.description: The story of how Lionel Messi v Cristiano Ronaldo became the rivalry that transformed football and defined a generation.

item.title: Liverpool will be enormous step for Iraola - Sutton
item.description: The Monday Night Club discuss Arne Slot's sacking from Liverpool and whether Andoni Iraola is the right replacement.

item.title: Argentina fans arrive after cycling to Kansas City
item.description: Three friends from Argentina arrive in Missouri in Kansas after cycling 10,500 miles from their home in Gualeguaychu to the venue of their team's opening game of the World Cup.

item.title: Tuchel tackles questions from team of under-11s
item.description: England head coach Thomas Tuchel fields questions from under-11 players before his team head to the World Cup.

item.title: Superstar in Spain, doubted in Brazil: Will Vinicius convince a nation?
item.description: Vinicius Junior is considered one of the best players in the world, but can he make the Brazilian public fall in love with him at the World Cup?

item.title: Bayern Munich interested in Liverpool winger Ngumoha
item.description: Bayern Munich are interested in signing Liverpool's 17-year-old winger Rio Ngumoha.

item.title: Fifa demands fans pay after ticketing error offered free World Cup tickets
item.description: Fifa says fans who were mistakenly given free tickets to World Cup group stage matches in Toronto must pay for them within seven days or lose the seats.

item.title: Wales aim to right their wrongs against the Czechs
item.description: Wales' trip to Montenegro did not go according to plan and, as a result, the path to the Women's World Cup is unchanged.

item.title: Real Madrid plan Olise bid - Saturday's gossip
item.description: Real Madrid are lining up a £130m move for Bayern Munich winger Michael Olise, Arne Slot turns down Fulham after being sacked by Liverpool, Newcastle United are close to making their first summer signing, plus more.

item.title: Tottenham sign Robertson after Liverpool exit
item.description: Tottenham complete the signing of Scotland captain Andy Robertson as he leaves Liverpool at the end of his contract.

item.title: Callaghan fumes at call to not give Turkey red card
item.description: Former Northern Ireland captain Marissa Callaghan says that Turkey striker Busem Seker "had to be sent off" after she controversially handled the ball before later scoring the winner during their 2-1 victory over Michael McArdle's side.

item.title: The anatomy of a Golden Boot winner - and who could win it in 2026?
item.description: A look at the common attributes of World Cup Golden Boot winners and the claims of the leading 2026 candidates examined.

item.title: Potter's Zlatan texts, overcoming failure & finding joy in Sweden
item.description: Graham Potter talks about his joy and pride in taking Sweden to the World Cup after hurt of Chelsea and West Ham dismissals.

item.title: Republic of Ireland have nothing to lose against France - Barrett
item.description: Republic of Ireland striker Amber Barrett says the side "are going to give everything we have" during Tuesday's match against France when they have the chance to secure automatic qualification for the World Cup.

item.title: How England can still qualify for the Women's World Cup
item.description: England, Wales, Scotland and Northern Ireland are all vying for places at the Women's World Cup - but how can they get there with one round of group games left in European qualifying?

item.title: Messi v Ronaldo: A rivalry that's dominated for two decades
item.description: Cristiano Ronaldo and Lionel Messi have been at the top of the game for the past 20 years, BBC Sport looks at a rivalry that has shaped football.

item.title: Stockport County appoint Rochdale boss McNulty
item.description: Stockport County appoint Rochdale boss Jim McNulty as their new manager on a three-year deal.

item.title: Perez promises biggest deal in Real Madrid's history
item.description: Florentino Perez promises to make a club-record offer for an unnamed "great player" if re-elected as Real Madrid president on Sunday.

item.title: From EFL to World Cup - 11 players to look out for
item.description: BBC Sport takes a look at 11 EFL players to watch out for at this summer's World Cup.

item.title: Who are the World Cup's record holders?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at the record holders in World Cup history ahead of the 2026 tournament

item.title: World Cup 2026: How to follow on the BBC
item.description: The biggest World Cup in history starts on 11 June and there are more ways to follow it on the BBC than ever before.

item.title: Download your World Cup 2026 wallchart
item.description: Get your essential piece of kit now by downloading the Fifa World Cup 2026 wallchart.

item.title: World Cup 2026: All 48 squads confirmed
item.description: All 48 confirmed squads for the 2026 World Cup in the US, Canada and Mexico.

item.title: All done deals in June 2026
item.description: Check out the significant signings and departures in the Premier League, Scottish Premiership, EFL and Women's Super League.

item.title: Mo Salah: Never Give Up
item.description: The story of Mo Salah’s unlikely rise to global stardom.

item.title: Joe Swash meets young dads breaking stigmas
item.description: Too often dismissed, these dads across the UK are quietly redefining fatherhood

item.title: What are the deadlines for World Cup squads?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains the deadlines by which teams must name their final squads for the 2026 World Cup

item.title: How much prize money have Premier League teams earned in Europe?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains how much Arsenal, Aston Villa and Crystal Palace have earned in Europe this season

item.title: Which club received most red cards in a Premier League season?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything looks at the teams who have received the most red cards in a Premier League season.

item.title: Who won the Premier League golden boot?
item.description: BBC's Ask Me Anything team explains who has won the Premier League golden boot and other individual awards.

item.title: Who are the oldest players to feature at a World Cup?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at who the oldest players are in the history of the World Cup

item.title: Who has qualified for next season's Champions League?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains which teams have already qualified for the 2026-27 edition of the Champions League.

item.title: Who has the most Premier League assists in a season?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team look at who has provided the most assists in the history of the Premier League.

item.title: The underrated impact of Scotland's back-shaving hype man
item.description: Liam Kelly looks perplexed after being told he has been singled out by some team-mates as Scotland's "hype man".

item.title: Advantage Scotland as they await Cuthbert injury news
item.description: Scotland stay top of their World Cup qualifying group on goal difference from Belgium amid fears of a serious injury to key midfielder Erin Cuthbert.

item.title: O'Neill set to continue as Celtic boss - but other issues still to be resolved
item.description: Martin O'Neill will continue as Celtic manager after leading the side to a Premiership and Scottish Cup double.

item.title: Hearts set for Chesnokov offers - gossip
item.description: Speculation around ins and outs at Celtic, Rangers step up right-back chase and two clubs are linked with Hearts winger.

item.title: McLeish given St Mirren chance - how have young Premiership coaches fared?
item.description: St Mirren have chosen youth over experience in sticking with interim manager Craig McLeish in the long term, but how have other young managers fared in the Scottish Premiership?

item.title: Spain expose England's flaws in painful defeat
item.description: England just needed to avoid defeat against Spain - instead they suffered their heaviest loss in 17 years, but it is not time to panic yet.

item.title: Wales aim to right their wrongs against the Czechs
item.description: Wales' trip to Montenegro did not go according to plan and, as a result, the path to the Women's World Cup is unchanged.

item.title: How England can still qualify for the Women's World Cup
item.description: England, Wales, Scotland and Northern Ireland are all vying for places at the Women's World Cup - but how can they get there with one round of group games left in European qualifying?

item.title: How Cornish miners brought football to Mexico
item.description: As Mexico prepares to become the first country to host or co-host the World Cup three times, BBC Sport looks at its Cornish footballing roots.

item.title: The summer a golden generation lost its shine
item.description: The summer of 2006 was meant to be England's moment. A world-class squad, celebrity partners and confident fans - the hype was huge and the fall was hard.

item.title: How Toone is navigating grief through football
item.description: England and Manchester United midfielder Ella Toone opens up about grief, football and preparing for her wedding without her dad in a new BBC documentary.

item.title: When football turned to penalties to end 'cruel' system
item.description: No-one knew what to expect at football's first official penalty shootout - and no-one wanted to go down in history as the first to miss.

item.title: Frank's downfall: Inside his struggle to get players and fans onboard
item.description: Thomas Frank has been sacked as Tottenham manager after just eight months in charge. BBC Sport's Sami Mokbel takes a closer look at his tenure.

item.title: Cages, crushes and stabbings - is European away safety getting worse?
item.description: BBC Sport explores the safety issues fans face when following their team across Europe.

item.title: The great number nine decline - where have England's strikers gone?
item.description: BBC Sport takes an in-depth look at why England lack centre-forward options behind Harry Kane - a far cry from when the likes of Les Ferdinand only won 17 caps.
